steps.title: Spot the unclosed parenthetical
steps.content: The phrase "mustang" (a wild horse) properly closes its parenthetical, but "rodeo" (a ______ leaves the parenthetical open—there's an opening parenthesis with no closing match.

steps.title: Close the parenthetical
steps.content: Rule: an opening parenthesis must be closed by a matching closing parenthesis. The sentence has "rodeo" ( with no closing ) yet. Reconstructed sentence with each choice: A) "rodeo" (a roundup, — ends with a comma, leaving the parenthetical open; the first example uses a closing parenthesis, not a comma; eliminated. B) "rodeo" (a roundup) — closes the parenthetical exactly as "(a wild horse)" does earlier; correct. C) "rodeo" (a roundup; — ends with a semicolon, leaving the parenthetical open; eliminated. D) "rodeo" (a roundup— — ends with an em-dash, leaving the parenthetical open and mixing punctuation styles; eliminated. Verdict: B is correct.
